text:
Overall rating: 1.2/5 Would take again (%): 8% Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.9 Top tags: Tough Grader, Graded by few things, Test Heavy

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE1010 If you are new to computer science, Python, or coding in general, be prepared to teach yourself a majority of the topics in this course.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE1010 For teaching such an introductory-level course, he expects you to know so much. The funny thing is, I didn't learn a single thing from this guy at all! The online software this course uses (ZyBooks) is the only reason why I passed. Not only does he not teach, but when you ask him questions, he is just so rude and dismissive. Take anyone but him.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE2500 Taking this class made me go to SHAW to try and better my mental health. Prof. Strimple without a doubt is the worst professor I've ever had in my lifetime. He never teaches the class in lectures, he just reads really bad slides and then talks about his life. He will also set up office hours; when you go nobody is there to assist you. Just awful.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE1010 Horrible professor, lectures were useless, so many useless assignments every week, his TA's had to correct his work and did most of the work. The final exam was nothing like the practice exams and did NOT help.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE1010 He's absolutely awful. His office hours are a waste of time and his lectures are a waste of breath. If you want to understand foundations of code in python by any means, find someone else to teach it. He's in there at the same level of comprehension as someone who simply knows how to spell the word python. 0/10 would absolutely never recommend.

Quality 3.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE2500 While I don't think he was quite as bad as some of these reviews make it seem, there is definitely room for improvement. He is definitely a harsh grader and will take points off for very minor mistakes. Regardless, I will say I do feel like I learned a lot from him and his class. Nice person too, don't be afraid to talk to him during office hours.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE2500 Horrible professor. Homework is difficult and hard to understand because of how he writes it. Talks too much without even teaching properly. Does examples in class and somehow will mix himself up and do it wrong. Grades horribly because he wants it to be exactly how he does it and any other way is wrong.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3150 1 is not low enough for this class made me want to die. This professor is nowhere near a professional and honestly should've chosen a different career. He doesn't know what he is teaching and you will struggle to comprehend the way this professor grades his assignment. I am warning everyone for your own sake do not take this course, thank me later

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3150 The capacities of this platform are insufficient to express the pure vitriol I hold for this caricature of a class. I'll withhold, simply because I do not think Strimple is inherently a bad man. That being said, he makes a Professor like Jerry Shi look like a shining example of what the CS Department should aspire to be. Ignominiously disgraceful.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE3200 I was very excited for 3200. BUT the lectures are difficult to follow because he makes errors constantly and most relied on videos he posted last year. Expect an announcement each class fixing errors he made. HW instructions are vague. Historically he cares more about catching students cheating than producing quality study material, do better pls

Overall rating: 3.5/5 Would take again (%): 59% Level of difficulty (1-5): 4.1 Top tags: Amazing lectures, Clear grading criteria, Hilarious

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3500 People say Aguiar's section is harder, but his grading is very forgiving. Homework is mostly completion if you follow the guidelines. On exams are as long as you follow the general formula, you'll get most points even if your solution is wrong. The final lets you recover earlier exam points. Attend lecture and do the homework and you'll be fine.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3500 Derek is very approachable and easy to talk to! His lectures can sometimes move too quickly or be unclear, but he records them and makes them available. He's also a lenient grader and fair, I got some problems completely wrong on the first exam and still got over half credit for them. If you're proactive and put in effort you'll do well.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3500 I would say Derek is a nice professor, but don't take 3500 with him, his section is way harder than other, but Derek cares his students and he is always there to help you, but the content is too hard and the weekly homework is absolutely overwhelming, it probably took me for a WHOLE WEEK to finish every time. Overall, nice professor, tough course.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3500 Although Derek Aguiar's sections of this class are harder and different from other professors, It is for your benefit. He is very enthusiastic with his teachings and genuinely wants to help you learn to prepare for your future. The homework's he gives are the best preparation for the exams and he is a generous grader.

Quality 3.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3500 If you put in a TON of work, you will learn a lot from this course, and it will be useful. However, it doesn't make sense how taking this class with Aguiar compared to other professors is so much more difficult. He is passionate and wants everyone to learn, yes. But this could almost be a separate course entirely compared to the other sections.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3500 I never actually wrote one of these before but I just had to for Derek. 3500 is just a hard course in general, the people rating him low aren't doing him justice just because they had a hard time. Derek teaches with passion and is a hilarious guy. Derek will tell you what category of problems are going to be on exams. Amazing Professor.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE1010 The homework in the latter half of the course was a bit difficult for me, but that is only because I've never taken CS before (and thus, it takes a bit of work to learn, just like any other class). Everything in the first half of the semester was really easy. TONS of extra credit opportunities during lab times -- take advantage of it!

Quality 3.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3500 3500 with Aguiar is one of the hardest courses in the undergrad curriculum. He even says that his section is harder than any other sections. But by the time your done you will be an algorithms god. He is also very available in office hours (which you will need) and the final makes up for the midterms. DON'T TAKE THIS CONCURRENTLY WITH 3100 OR 3666

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3500 The homework is insanely hard and the exams are as well. he does not curve the class but has a different system at the end but that sucks because It barely brings your grade up or might not even. he also made a failing grade below a 50 which is the only good thing hes done. also hes easy to reach out too and sometimes is understanding.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3500 Derek is the best! 3500 is just a hard class, but I have never seen a professor care so much about student's success. He really wants you to understand the content and do well. Derek grades very fairly, gives a ton of partial credit, answers emails and discord quickly (literally at like 1a.m. sometimes). One of the best professor's I've had here.

Overall rating: 4.7/5 Would take again (%): 93% Level of difficulty (1-5): 2.6 Top tags: Amazing lectures, Gives Good Feedback, Caring

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 1.0 | CSE2102 I didn't have him for CSE 2102 but his slides were used for my class. His materials prepared me beyond what I could have asked for in my Software Engineering Internship. Pay attention in his class and it WILL benefit you. Legend.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE2102 10/10 would highly recommend. If you are looking for a class to help kickstart your software engineering journey, take CSE 2102 with Professor Clark. He taught us many of the tools and techniques used in the real world. My favorite CSE class at UConn so far.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2102 He is a very good professor who cares a lot about the students. Group project was the main part of the class which was good because you learn a lot from it. One thing I would say is that the lectures can be very long so you can lose focus very easily, but other than that the class was good.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2102 Great and very understanding professor. This course is most applicable in the 'real world'. While the group projects emphasize teamwork, you are graded as an individual, and how much work you do. The group project was difficult at times but taught me a ton about software engineering.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE2102 Clark is a real software engineer and has real world experience. His class is very informative and gives a lot of insight on the field. He truly cares about his students, will stay late after class to help and is very accessible on Discord. He's one of the most respectable and kind professors at UCONN. Highly recommend Clark, would take again.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2102 Participation quizzes every lecture, 3 hws, 2 exams, labs graded on participation. Throughout the semester, you work towards a final project. Everyone is kept on the same page with graded weekly milestones. Learning expectations are simple. Learn in lab, and demonstrate in next milestone/HW. Very available, stays late if needed.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE4095 AWS Cloud Computing special topics course. Lectures were very applicable to industry work and connected back to current topics from data breaches to web development. Each lecture usually had a demo which was pretty helpful when it came to completing the homework. Lots of hands-on work.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2102 Unlike other computer science teachers, this guy is a *real* software engineer who is doing *real* software engineering *right now.* This means his lectures are extremely relevant, and even if some may complain that they're boring, I promise that they will come in handy if you ever get a job in the tech space. Plus, he's just a great guy overall!

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2102 Greatest thing to happen to UConn CSE department. Jon is a gem and is seriously passionate about teaching his students. Both the class and group project were also super fun. I feel like I should have definitely worked harder though because the curve was very merciful and had I focused more it would have been an easy A.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE2102 The 2102 course rework is incredibly useful and gives great insight into the actual industry. Majority of your grade is based on the group project and as long as you work with your group and know the ins and outs of the project, you'll do fine in the course. Lecture was very boring but key terms at end of each are what was important for exams.

Overall rating: 4.5/5 Would take again (%): 89% Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.6 Top tags: Caring, Helpful, Tough Grader

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 1.0 | CSE2050 Justin is a new professor but good at explaining and very knowledgable about what he does. My only complaint is that occasionally announcement were made exclusively on Discord and not emailed, which lead to me along with half my lecture coming in confused on the first cancellation.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2050 Absolutely goated professor! Not only does he know the material, but he is willing to teach you more about Python and give opportunities to become an even better programmer. If you are struggling in this course, the best tips I would say is to come in during office hours, do the practice tests, and look for resources and videos online. Good luck!

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2050 Justin more than teaches, he helps you understand and apply the true purpose of the content. He knows everything inside and out, and is incredibly helpful at breaking down any problem/difficulty, explaining it in a simple to comprehend and apply way. He is an awesome person too, with enjoyable lectures. If given the option, I'd take him every time.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3150 Of all the computer science professors I have taken at UConn, he stands above the rest. It felt like he was the first professor who cared about your knowledge of computer science and success post grad. For those stating that exams weigh too much, suck it up. It's the department's solution to cheating instead of letting AI prompt engineers graduate.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 1.0 | CSE3150 He was lenient with grading, available after class, and took student feedback seriously. Put at least a little bit of effort into the class, don't overuse AI, ask him questions, and actually remember material from 3100 to be successful in his class. Highly recommend him as a professor

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3150 Exams shouldn't outweigh projects by such a large margin in this course. CS should enhance students' ability to reasonably utilize AI to help them complete large-scale projects with thousands of lines of code, rather than having them lose all points for a question in exam just because they forgot a symbol and the code couldn't be compiled.

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3150 Nice person.The exams make up a large portion of the overall grade. The syllabus also states that scoring below 60 on any single exam results in failing the course. I found the grading policy difficult to understand, especially with changes during the semester. If I had other options, I might have chosen a different course.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3150 I was unable to get into the class this fall but ended up continuing to go to the lectures because it was just a really good class. He knows the content backwards and forwards and has good feel for how students perceive the material. He saw me outside class and gave me material/help in the course even though I was not enrolled. Great instructor.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3500 He is knowledgeable and kind, but CSE 3500 with him is stressful. Its all programming, no proofs, to prepare you for interviews in the industry. He grades strictly and exams are worth 80 percent of the grade. If you want a challenge or improve programming skills I would recommend, otherwise go with someone else!

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3500 A great professor who I've learned loads from! UConn does a pretty poor ob of making sure their CS students are competent programmers, and Justin is fixing that in my opinion. I enjoyed the class, and it was one of the few I've taken where I felt like the knowledge was worth what I was paying for.

Overall rating: 4.4/5 Would take again (%): 86% Level of difficulty (1-5): 4.1 Top tags: Amazing lectures, Hilarious, Accessible outside class

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE1729 1729 is a tough class, but Professor Michel managed to simplify the concepts, and was very engaging. He expects a lot out of students, based on difficulty of HW and the exams, but the curves are more than fair. Exams are built to test your actual coding ability, so rigorously studying the textbook and notes may not save you, if you can't code well.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE1729 Professor Michel clearly expects a lot out of his students, as evidenced by his exams which are extremely hard. Yet at the same time, his generous curves demonstrate that he understands how insanely hard the exams are. His lectures are very engaging and he does very well at explaining concepts that are both difficult to learn and to teach.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2100 I got an A out of this class, but had to work pretty hard for it. Significant curve as my actual average was a 78. Be ready for a lot of long nights doing projects. Start early. If you do well on the homeworks and merely pass the exams, you'll get an A.

Quality 3.5 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE2100 Very tough class. Got a 20% on the midterm (and I have an A- homework/project average). Still definitely learned a lot and he's willing to help you if you ask, he just expects a lot from his students.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3100 I almost failed the class because of how difficult it was, however he is one of the best lecturers at the University. He runs an extremely tight ship in terms of assignments and exams. The first exam average was around a 20%, and half the class got a 0 on the second exam, including myself. At least 50% dropped it.

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3100 Laurent definitely knows his stuff but his expectations are unbelievably high. Exams were not on par with homework. For the most part, partial credit wasn't given on homework. If the program worked incorrectly, sometimes credit was given. If the program didn't compile, no credit was given (this was also true for exams which were in person coding).

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3160 Good teaching, can go very fast at times but not an issue if you ask or get help outside of class. Cares for your success. He says you will do good if you practice and that's definitely true. Attending class helps but only if you actually try to follow the slides. The slides are fine.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3160 Laurent's courses are very difficult, but he makes up for that by being a really good professor. He cares a lot about your success and is always willing to help if you're putting in the effort. I recommend taking a course with him if you have the chance.

Quality 3.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE4100 Class difficulty grows exponentially with time. Do well on early hw assignments, so your hw average stays high. He is also willing to extend assignments if u ask. He drops two lowest hw assignments, which is great. The exams are tough. You need to study and practice a lot if you want to do good on them. Gives some small exam hints in lectures.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 1.0 | CSE4102 Very good professor. Teaches course material in a very clear and appropriate manner. Expects you to EARN your grade. Exams are hard (as others have mentioned) but if you put in the hours necessary for practicing the material and doing the assignments *independently* then an A is definitely attainable

Overall rating: 4.7/5 Would take again (%): 94% Level of difficulty (1-5): 2.8 Top tags: Amazing lectures, Group projects, Mandatory attendance

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE2050 she's so sweet! her lectures were super helpful and gave us a lot of grading opportunities (labs, participation, group project, and exams). definitely would recommend to anyone taking the course.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 1.0 | CSE2050 Lina Kloub is an amazing professor, and honestly, one of the best instructors I have come across yet.She teaches concepts visually and makes each lecture engaging. The course does feel a bit heavy with several different assignments, but I wish I could have her as a professor for every single CS course I have to take in the future-she is that great!

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2050 While the exams might seem a little difficult, they were fair, and all material she goes over in the slides and lecture. No homework, and we are given ample amount of time to finish our labs.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2050 She is highly organized, posting all lecture notes, code, and even videos on Blackboard. She is also approachable and patient, and most importantly, she has a strong ability to break down complex ideas into manageable pieces that are easy to understand and retain. Great teacher, overall no complaints.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE3500 Professor Kloub taught a very hard class but made it very easy and simple. Going to lectures helped make it way easier. Also, all the notes were on huskyct so it was easy to review and practice.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE3500 Professor Kloub is great! Active and engaged inside and outside the classroom. Genuinely wants to see her students succeed, and balances out exams with lots of project grades. I'm usually not a great test taker, but her study materials were great and exams were manageable. Homeworks were a reasonable length.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3500 I loved Professor Kloub's class! Her slides and lectures were very informative, and the tests were fine if you did the in-class worksheets and homeworks and understood them. Overall, I would definitely recommend!

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3500 This class was so hard. no opportunities for extra credit, TAs grade horrible, harshly, no partial credit ever given, exams are difficult— not enough prep for them. They don't care about you passing or not, do not recommend her at all. No curves either. Never seen cs professor not curve knowing it's hardest major + one of hardest classes at UConn.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2102 Kloub's teaching of this course was probably the best course I've taken at UConn so far. The class made sense with her lectures, and the TA review for the midterms was also really helpful. I enjoyed the Project-Based Learning aspect of the class, giving us experience building a full-stack project. The labs are actually helpful and applicable.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE2102 I would absolutely have Lina as my professor for all CS courses! She's one of the best professors I have had and it feels embarrassing that I didn't have her for CSE2050 or CSE3500. Her CSE2102 is an easy class and she's pretty flexible. The labs, the homeworks, and the group project weren't too difficult but did require effort. I recommend her.

Overall rating: 2.2/5 Would take again (%): 30% Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.2 Top tags: Lots of homework, Get ready to read, Lectures not helpful

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE1010 Lectures are not helpful at all, grade is extreamly exam heavy with average being in the 60s. If you do not have previous CS experience you will struggle.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE1010 She is not a very understanding professor, was unkind and frankly rude to me when discussing basic accommodations involving the CSD when I have had zero issues with any other professor. There is homework due literally every 2 days for no good reason just not a good experience overall.

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE1010 The class itself was ok, and for the most part was easy. As long as you do the zybooks, you should do ok on the exams. However, the zybooks do take forever to do, so don't wait till the last minute to do them. Her lectures were pretty pointless as she would just babble about random stuff the whole time. Labs were an easy 100.

Quality 3.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE1010 This class is relatively difficult and homework heavy. The hw is online and is quite tedious but helps substantially in understanding the hard material. Attendance is not mandatory neither are the labs but they should be completed as they account for a good part of the grade. Overall, if you do the zybooks hw and try to understand it, its not bad.

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE1010 Zybooks homework are absolutely ridiculous. Exams get progressively more difficult as you go through the course and very minimal extra credit. If you haven't coded before it is very difficult to keep up. Olga is a very nice and understanding professor, I just think its too difficult for an introductory course.

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2500 She's hard to follow. There's homework but its all on zybooks and it isn't helpful at all when it comes to the tests. 2500 is already hard and she didn't do much to make it easier. Even if you go to her privately and ask questions, she isn't helpful. She can be a bit rude too.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2500 She is unbelievably nice and passionate about her classes. Seriously, go talk to her about anything in the class. Additionally she helped cut me a deal that saved my grade. If you put in effort and match her energy, she will be one of the best professors you will ever have. Still, I think her lectures are a bit tough to follow

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 2.0 | CSE2500 Goated professor. Her lectures and class activities are engaging, and she really cares about helping students understand the material. You'll learn a lot just by participating, and the examples in class are often directly related to the exams. Super nice, too.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2500 The exams were not graded fairly in my opinion , the TAs were harsh graders too. I did not really like the way lectures were taught.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2500 5 exams which was really annoying got to a point where we had one every 3 lectures. 1 got dropped. I'd say she was a good teacher and easy homework was a large part of the grade.

Overall rating: 4.3/5 Would take again (%): 86% Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.6 Top tags: Caring, Tough grader, Amazing lectures

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2050 He was a very good professor who despite being new motivated his students.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2050 He's pretty understanding of circumstances that may prevent work from getting done, and allows you to make it up if you have a valid reason for it. He's accessible outside of class, and even in the lectures he often asks for input on his teaching style, to see if it actually helps us learn.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE2050 Professor Jignaas Pattipati brings great energy to class, and really encourages his students to participate in lecture by asking/answering questions, which creates an environment conducive to learning. My only "complaint" (which I found not to be that bad) was the fact that he clicks through the lecture slides really fast, but to each their own.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3100 Best prof for CSE3100. If you are willing to learn, you will gain a lot. He is passionate and truly cares about student success. He prepares you for more than just exams. He is very strict about ai,do not use it, you will get caught. Sometimes explanations can be unclear, but he is very accessible outside of class and helpful in office hours

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3100 He clearly cares about his teaching but he doesn't actually do anything in lecture to help students. Lot's of extra credit opportunities and he is very strict about AI usage. He has potential but he cannot lecture to save his life so I had to learn everything myself.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3100 The course along with the exams and hw are pretty tough however Swamy knows this and takes it into account while grading. Show up to lecture, pay attention and you'll be rewarded. Dude is hilarious too, always looked forward to lecture cause it was like going to a comedy club. Don't use AI, code on your own, ask for help.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3100 Swamy is a chill guy, his lectures are really good. Takes the time to answer questions. Exams are hard asf but he is forgiving with his grading for the most part, just don't cheat or get caught or he WILL get you. If you go to lecture and take time to do the homework you should be fine.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3100 People that are giving awful ratings either got caught cheating, or only use AI on HW (btw Wei Wei makes the HW). I took this class in Fall 24 and although he can get lectures done pretty quick, he went the extra mile with sample code and was really understanding when I got sick before the first exam with catching me up and rescheduling.

Quality 3.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3100 Overall not a bad professor but it's a hard class that no matter what you need to put effort if you want to get a decent grade. Exams are extremely difficult and formatted to catch cheating. Super strict on Ai/cheating. Very overhyped in my opinion and can be rude but as long as you take the time to learn the material you should be fine.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3140 Swamy is a goated lecturer. His lectures are interesting and engaging, w/slides posted on HuskyCT. However, his grading can be tough. For exams, write simple answers that go into depth but not too much as extra details can be marked wrong. At the time of this review, an exam replacement was offered for the final; don't bank on it, though. GL!

Overall rating: 2/5 Would take again (%): 27% Level of difficulty (1-5): 4.2 Top tags: Lots of Homework, Tough grader, Get ready to read

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3000 Its actually absurd how strict he is with grading for a 1 credit class. I'm getting grades in the range of 60s-90s for a paper that is supposed to be opinion based. For the final discussion he cut points out of the grade because we were missing stuff, but he also cut us off early for time purposes when we were getting to that part of the project.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3000 I'd avoid this professor if possible. The weekly readings total up to over 100 pages with a one-page reflection paper every week. Vague instructions for group project, and during presentations he was unhelpful and even rude when students had technical issues. The attendance policy is also extreme missing three classes means you fail the course.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3000 3 credits worth of work for a 1 credit class. Not only does he assign weekly one page papers (all mandatory) with incredibly vague guidelines, but he then grades them based entirely on vibes, with no rubric. On top of that, the same attendance policy as a lab course plus weekly online discussion made this class an absolute nightmare to have taken.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE2500 Not the worst professor but not my first choice. He definitely wants us to learn but he's rough with his exam problems and material taught. If you put time in and ask questions you'll for sure do well, especially with the extra credit and partial grading. His office hours were pretty late so that's a downside.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3150 Lowkey hates his students. Assigns a LOT of DIFFICULT work, will hide tests in the autograder to trip you up. Low quality lectures. Does not want to curve when the exam average was 51. Will teach new content 10 minutes before the exam. Will not finish attendance assignments in class so you have to do them yourself. Obsessed with catching cheaters.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3150 Probably the worst CS professor at UConn. Extremely difficult exams with no curve. The average was a failing grade and there's no curve, he refuses to take fault and blames the class for not trying. This man cannot speak English, or any language honestly has a really bad stutter and a ton of homework. Egotistical with no social awareness.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3500 He was a bit hard to understand, respectfully, because of his thick accent. There was one occasion where I wasn't understanding him and went to him after class. He was surprised I didn't understand a certain concept and basically kicked me out refusing to clarify anything else. I would say he ranks in the top 3 of the worst professors I've had.

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3150 Tough class. The prof has videos of another prof's lectures and told us to watch those instead. The coding HWs are vague and need more clarification. Final project was a mess in terms of delivery and deadlines, autograders not working. His lecturing skills are not the best, it is better to watch O.O. design pattern videos from YT instead of him.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3150 I would take most of these negative reviews with a grain of salt. Good professor and a fair grader. There are weekly assignments that teach you a lot. No final exam; final project was relatively straightforward - you will need to dedicate a solid few hours to it. You'll succeed if you focus on learning, not just completing assignments.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3150 Uses other prof lectures outside of class, lots of homework, unclear directions, lots of out of pocket comments. Lack of communication with TAs (hard to get any answers, a TA mentioned they only had one meeting the entire semester). Tried to make an entire new project with dates overlapping with exam, refuses to take advice and input from students.

Overall rating: 2.5/5 Would take again (%): 40% Level of difficulty (1-5): 4.2 Top tags: Test heavy, Lots of homework, Participation matters

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE3666 Wow, what an underrated professor. I was afraid to take this class with him as I had a conflict with Scoggin's, but I had nothing to worry about. Shi does a great job presenting the content, answering questions, and stimulating interest in computer architecture. Give him a chance, REALLY participate, and you will see him as I do!

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3666 What a waste of time and money. Lectures are bad, along with slides, HW and labs. Tough exams weighted 70% with 50 to 60% averages. No curve. No effort is put into improving the course.

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3666 NEVER TAKE 3666 WITH THIS GUY. exams are weighting 70% of your overall grade, so you could pass this course without attending the lad and lecture and you could forget about the lab and homework if you can do good on the exams, and he is always mumbling during the lecture, he just enjoy watching students suffer. worst CS professor ever.

Quality 4.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3666 His exams are straightforward and cover exactly what you'd expect based on the material. The lectures are pretty clear. He is not that bad.

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3666 The one comment on here that is 5 stars is from one of the TA's This is a hard class. Jerry Shi was involved in a shady scandal 10 years ago. He is hard to understand during lecture and it's required. He hates answering questions. We were promised a final grade curve, but after the final, he just ghosted anyone who asked about it.

Quality 2.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3666 This class is bad, but Jerry makes it worse. I would show up to the other professor's lectures because I literally could not understand Jerry's rambling. He's not an evil person and his exams aren't tougher, but you won't get anything out of his lectures because he doesn't explain anything well, his extra credit was harder, and his curve was less.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 1.0 | CSE3666 Boy oh boy, where do I even begin? Professor Jerry Shi, my guiding light. I have admired you ever since I first attended your Computer Science Architecture class. The way you break down complex systems, it's like watching a master artist at work. If only I could freeze time, so I'd never have to witness the day you decide to retire from teaching.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 4.0 | CSE3100 Jerry is actually a solid CSE professor. He teaches too fast sometimes, but my god is he accessible thru piazza like 24/7. Exams are, imo, easier than cse2050 and homeworks aren't too difficult. He uploads demo code to his Github which is nice to reference. I didn't have high expectations for Jerry but was pleasantly surprised and would reccommend!

Quality 1.0 | Difficulty 5.0 | CSE3400 The number of things wrong with this class are kind of incredible. Absolutely terrible lectures, as the professor struggles to form even a single coherent thought, leaving every student learning absolutely nothing. Slides are atrocious, and the only textbook referenced is the worst resource they could have chosen. Genuinely disappointing. Avoid.

Quality 5.0 | Difficulty 3.0 | CSE4302 Jerry is very friendly and has a good sense of humor. You can tell he actually cares about whether you learn the subject matter. He will test you in class to make sure you comprehend, and drill it into your head if you don't. The homeworks were actually helpful and let you practice the concepts you learned in class.
